content.full_text:
January 13, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95804

RE:     Request for Advice
        Statement of Economic Interest Form 730

Gentlemen:

I was elected to the Clearlake Project Area Committee (PAC) in 1988.  I have major concerns about some recent actions we have been instructed to take and would appreciate your opinion on the following:

1.     As a member of the PAC I was instructed to file an Assuming Office Statement of Economic Interest (Form 730) within thirty days of assuming office, which I did.

2.     I have recently been instructed by the city clerk to fill out a Form 730 for leaving office by city officials because the city council voted to dissolve the Clearlake PAC last Fall.

3.     I have not as yet filled out that form.  I feel the dissolving of the Clearlake PAC may not have been legally correct, and I wish to avoid any potential fines or disciplinary actions from the FPPC or other agencies which may occur should this be confirmed.

4.     City officials have told all appointed Redevelopment Advisory Committee members (PAC) (which replaced the PAC elected/appointed committee) that they must fill out the same Form 730 even though the city's staff maintains that the PAC members are not subject to FPPC regulations.  The city intends to keep the completed PAC forms for informational purposes only as public record.

5.     At the 1/11/90 PAC meeting I questioned the validity of the whole procedure and asked that the Redevelopment Agency Special Counsel provide a legal opinion on the matter.

6.     I was told that the same person would be responding to my request as had on previous occasions, and that the answer would probably be the same as before, so why bother.

1

I then asked to have these matters agendized for the
next RAC meeting on 1/27/90, and was informed that we had already
gone through this once and I was just trying to slow up the
process.

3. Not knowing the procedure I was to follow (since we had
not adopted any guidelines about agendas), I then made a motion
to have these matters placed on the next agenda because I was
still extremely concerned about violating state laws and
regulations. The motion was not seconded and I was told that the
issue, in effect, was dead.

4. Even though I have asked, I have yet to receive any
statutory evidence to support the actions I am being told to
undertake by city officials and staff.

Therefore, I respectfully request an opinion from the FPPC as to:

A. If members are appointed by legislative action of the
city council to either a Project Area Committee or a
Redevelopment Advisory Committee, do they come under the
jurisdiction of the FPPC?

B. If I currently have a Form 730 on file with the city of
Clearlake and the FPPC, do I have to refile that form just
because the city council changed the name of the committee?

C. If members of the RAC are not subject to the FPPC
regulations, why should we be required to fill out the Form 730?
Aren't these forms part of FPPC regulations?

It is my belief that the members of the RAC (now called RAC) have
been given erroneous advice on FPPC and Brown Act issues by both
Clearlake city officials and their legal counsel in an attempt
to eliminate the safeguards provided to low- and moderate-income
citizens of Clearlake who may be displaced as a result of some of
the proposed redevelopment projects. (RACs must be formed if such
displacement occurs according to redevelopment law.)

I was duly elected by the citizens within the Project Area
boundaries, and I feel I have a mandated responsibility to assure
their rights are protected. I cannot fulfill that responsibility
if other public officials and city staff including special
counsels, continue to provide misleading and erroneous advice
which we are expected to use as a foundation for our decisions
as members of either the Project Area Committee or the Redevelopment Advisory Committee.

I have been informed of other inquiries submitted to the FPPC in
recent months which address additional concerns about the actions
of Clearlake city officials regarding the entire redevelopment
process over the past year. I support further investigation into
those matters, as well.

2

Thank you for your time and consideration of this letter.   Since
city officials and appointed committees continue to press forward
with  decisions based on what I and many others consider to be a
defective legal foundation, I would look forward to a  thorough
and timely investigation.

Sincerely,

Gary Howard Allen
14802 Uhl Avenue
Clearlake CA 95422
707-994-5004

cc:  Lake County District Attorney

3

January 11, 1980


CLEARLAKE REDEVELOPMENT ADVISORY COMMITTEE


To Whom It May Concern:

     On November 27,1989 at the Clearlake Project Area Committee
Meeting in which we were only to discuss only the CONTINUED DISCUSSION
OF THE (PAC), I would like to ask these questions to the Chairman
and Legal Council.

1.  [ The Breakup Of PAC ]  It was not on the Agenda.  This means it
was not an Agendized Item and should have not even been discussed.
Why and How do you feel we were able to take any Action to dissolve
(PAC)?  I feel the actions were not legal.

2.  The procedures for forming PAC had no procedures for the dis-
solving of PAC.  Will you be looking into this matter ?  I feel
this matted also need s looking into before we go on to far..

3.  I would like to know what procedures need to be in place before
the City of Clearlake can take any Action to dissolve PAC?  This
means the cities resolution needs to be amended ahead of time.  The
resolution that I am referring to is the resolution on forming PAC.

     Information and discussion with Staff at California Redevelop-
ment Agency Association in Sacramento leads me to believe we should
look into these matters to see if our previous procedures and action
were done properly and legally.  I feel we all should look into
these matters now that I have tried to bring them to every ones
attention .

     I feel this body should still be as a PAC for the City of
Clearlake like it was intended.  Even as a Redevelopment Advisory
Committee you and I are still subject to FPPC.

                                        Sincerely,
                                        [signature]
                                        Gary H. Allen

     (Elected member of PAC now re-formed as RAC)
